---
title: "4.1.1 System software and application software"
subtitle: "Application software examples · P149"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        .reveal .term { color: #cf1f2e; font-weight: 700; }
        .reveal .mark { background: #fff36d; padding: 0 0.08em; }
        .reveal .tight-list li { margin-bottom: 0.24em; }
        .reveal .figure-note { font-size: 0.82em; line-height: 1.28; }
        .reveal .figure-placeholder { border: 1px dashed #8aa0bf; border-radius: 8px; background: #f4f8ff; color: #40526b; padding: 1.1em 1.3em; margin: 0.8em 0; font-size: 0.9em; text-align: center; }
        .reveal .clean-table table { font-size: 0.74em; line-height: 1.1; }
        .reveal .clean-table th, .reveal .clean-table td { padding: 0.24em 0.34em; vertical-align: top; }
        </style>
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 4 Directory / 返回 Unit 4 目录](../../index.html)

## Application Software

### Specific task software

<span class="term">Application software</span> refers to programs that allow the user to do specific tasks.

The textbook gives several examples of typical application software.

::: {.figure-placeholder}
Figure 4.3 Application software screenshot placeholder
:::

## Documents, Numbers and Data

### Office-style applications

::: {.clean-table}
| Software | Used for |
|---|---|
| <span class="term">Word processor</span> | Manipulating a text document, such as an essay or a report |
| <span class="term">Spreadsheet</span> | Organising and manipulating numerical data |
| <span class="term">Database</span> | Organising, manipulating and analysing data |
:::

Typical functions include editing text, using formulas, producing graphs, carrying out database queries, and adding, deleting or modifying data.

## Control and Measuring Software

### Interfacing with sensors

<span class="term">Control and measuring software</span> allows a computer or microprocessor to interface with sensors.

It can:

::: {.tight-list}
- measure physical quantities in the real world, such as temperatures
- control applications by comparing <span class="mark">sensor data</span> with stored data
- send signals to alter process parameters, for example opening a valve to change pH
:::

## Apps

### Mobile and tablet applications

<span class="term">Apps</span> normally refer to software which runs on mobile phones or tablets.

They are normally downloaded from an App Store and range from games to sophisticated software such as phone banking.

Common examples include video and music streaming, GPS and camera facilities.

## Editing Software

### Photos, video and graphics

::: {.clean-table}
| Software | Used for |
|---|---|
| <span class="term">Photo editing software</span> | Manipulating digital photographs, such as brightness, contrast or colour saturation |
| <span class="term">Video editing software</span> | Manipulating videos to produce a new video |
| <span class="term">Graphics manipulation software</span> | Changing bitmap and vector images |
:::

## Classroom Check

### Give examples of application software

A complete answer should include:

::: {.tight-list}
- application software performs <span class="mark">specific user tasks</span>
- word processors, spreadsheets and databases have different purposes
- control and measuring software can interface with sensors
- apps normally run on mobile phones or tablets
- editing software can manipulate photos, video, bitmap images or vector images
:::

## Bridge

### Next: System software and utilities

The next deck returns to system software examples and utility programs.

## End

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 4 Directory / 返回 Unit 4 目录](../../index.html)
